---
title: "denet example in an alignment workflow"
output:
  html_document:
    fig_caption: true
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE,
                      fig.width = 12, fig.height = 8)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(jsonlite)
library(patchwork)
library(scales)
library(stringr)
```

```{r load-benchmark-tsv}
step_labels <- c(
  simulate_genome = "Simulate genome",
  index_genome    = "Index genome",
  simulate_reads  = "Simulate reads",
  align           = "Align reads",
  sort_bam        = "Sort BAM",
  index_bam       = "Index BAM",
  markdup         = "Mark duplicates",
  index_markdup   = "Index markdup",
  faidx_genome    = "Faidx genome",
  call_variants   = "Call variants"
)

steps <- names(step_labels)

ts_facet_labels <- c(
  "Simulate reads" = "Simulate\nreads",
  "Align reads" = "Align\nreads",
  "Sort BAM" = "Sort\nBAM",
  "Mark duplicates" = "Mark\nduplicates",
  "Call variants" = "Call\nvariants"
)

read_benchmarks <- function(outdir, condition) {
  lapply(steps, function(step) {
    path <- file.path(outdir, "benchmarks", paste0(step, ".tsv"))
    df <- read.table(path, header = TRUE, sep = "\t")
    df$step <- step
    df$condition <- condition
    df$repeat_id <- seq_len(nrow(df))
    df
  }) |> bind_rows()
}

baseline     <- read_benchmarks("results_baseline",    "Snakemake")
denet_wrap   <- read_benchmarks("results_denet",        "Snakemake + denet wrap")
denet_native <- read_benchmarks("results_denet_native", "Snakemake + denet native")

condition_levels <- c("Snakemake", "Snakemake + denet wrap", "Snakemake + denet native")

bench <- bind_rows(baseline, denet_wrap, denet_native) |>
  mutate(
    step_label = factor(step_labels[step], levels = step_labels),
    condition  = factor(condition, levels = condition_levels)
  )
```

```{r load-denet-jsonl}
parse_denet_jsonl <- function(path) {
  lines <- readLines(path)
  # first line is metadata (has "pid" key), remaining lines are samples
  samples <- lines[sapply(lines, function(l) grepl('"ts_ms"', l))]
  parsed <- lapply(samples, function(l) {
    obj <- fromJSON(l)
    agg <- obj$aggregated
    data.frame(
      ts_ms         = agg$ts_ms,
      cpu_usage     = agg$cpu_usage,
      mem_rss_mb    = agg$mem_rss_kb / 1024,
      disk_read_mb  = agg$disk_read_bytes / 1024^2,
      disk_write_mb = agg$disk_write_bytes / 1024^2,
      thread_count  = agg$thread_count
    )
  })
  df <- bind_rows(parsed)
  # relative time from start of first sample
  df$time_s <- (df$ts_ms - min(df$ts_ms)) / 1000
  df
}

denet_metrics_dir <- "results_denet/denet_metrics"

denet_files <- list.files(denet_metrics_dir, pattern = "\\.jsonl$", full.names = TRUE)

denet_ts <- lapply(denet_files, function(f) {
  step <- str_remove(basename(f), "\\.jsonl$")
  # metadata lines start with {"pid":...; sample lines start with {"ts_ms":...
  # (samples also contain "pid" inside children[], so match on line prefix)
  lines <- readLines(f)
  meta_idx <- which(grepl('^\\{"pid":', lines))
  run_list <- lapply(seq_along(meta_idx), function(i) {
    start <- meta_idx[i] + 1
    end   <- if (i < length(meta_idx)) meta_idx[i + 1] - 1 else length(lines)
    block <- lines[start:end]
    block <- block[nzchar(block) & grepl('^\\{"ts_ms":', block)]
    if (length(block) == 0) return(NULL)
    parsed <- lapply(block, function(l) {
      obj <- fromJSON(l)
      agg <- obj$aggregated
      data.frame(
        ts_ms = as.numeric(agg$ts_ms),
        cpu_usage = as.numeric(agg$cpu_usage),
        mem_rss_mb = as.numeric(agg$mem_rss_kb) / 1024,
        disk_write_mb = as.numeric(agg$disk_write_bytes) / 1024^2
      )
    })
    df <- bind_rows(parsed) |> arrange(ts_ms)
    df$time_s <- (df$ts_ms - df$ts_ms[1]) / 1000
    df$repeat_id <- i
    df
  })
  df <- bind_rows(run_list)
  df$step <- step
  df
}) |> bind_rows() |>
  mutate(step_label = factor(step_labels[step], levels = step_labels))
```

```{r fig-wall-time}
# n_repeats is read from data so the subtitle stays accurate if benchmark_repeats changes
n_repeats <- bench |>
  distinct(step_label, condition, repeat_id) |>
  count(step_label, condition) |>
  pull(n) |>
  max()

bench_summary <- bench |>
  group_by(step_label, condition) |>
  summarise(
    mean_s = mean(s),
    sd_s   = sd(s),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Error bars show +/- 1 SD across repeats.
# SD (rather than SE or CV) is used because we want to convey the absolute
# run-to-run spread in seconds, not the precision of the mean estimate.
p_time <- ggplot(bench_summary,
       aes(x = step_label, y = mean_s, fill = condition,
           ymin = pmax(mean_s - sd_s, 0), ymax = mean_s + sd_s)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(0.75), width = 0.65) +
  geom_errorbar(position = position_dodge(0.75), width = 0.25) +
  scale_y_continuous(trans = "log1p", labels = label_number(accuracy = 0.1)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Snakemake" = "#4C72B0",
                               "Snakemake + denet wrap" = "#DD8452",
                               "Snakemake + denet native" = "#55A868")) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Wall-clock time (s)", fill = NULL,
       title = "A  Wall-clock time per step",
       subtitle = paste0("mean +/- 1 SD, n = ", n_repeats, " repeats")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))
```

```{r fig-max-rss}
rss_summary <- bench |>
  group_by(step_label, condition) |>
  summarise(
    mean_rss = mean(max_rss),
    sd_rss   = sd(max_rss),
    .groups  = "drop"
  )

p_rss <- ggplot(rss_summary,
       aes(x = step_label, y = mean_rss, fill = condition,
           ymin = pmax(mean_rss - sd_rss, 0), ymax = mean_rss + sd_rss)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(0.75), width = 0.65) +
  geom_errorbar(position = position_dodge(0.75), width = 0.25) +
  scale_y_continuous(trans = "log1p", labels = label_number(accuracy = 1)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Snakemake" = "#4C72B0",
                               "Snakemake + denet wrap" = "#DD8452",
                               "Snakemake + denet native" = "#55A868")) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Peak RSS (MB)", fill = NULL,
       title = "B  Peak resident memory per step",
       subtitle = paste0("mean +/- 1 SD, n = ", n_repeats, " repeats")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))
```

```{r fig-io-out}
io_summary <- bench |>
  group_by(step_label, condition) |>
  summarise(
    mean_io = mean(io_out),
    sd_io = sd(io_out),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

p_io_bar <- ggplot(io_summary,
       aes(x = step_label, y = mean_io, fill = condition,
           ymin = pmax(mean_io - sd_io, 0), ymax = mean_io + sd_io)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(0.75), width = 0.65) +
  geom_errorbar(position = position_dodge(0.75), width = 0.25) +
  scale_y_continuous(trans = "log1p", labels = label_number(accuracy = 1)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Snakemake" = "#4C72B0",
                               "Snakemake + denet wrap" = "#DD8452",
                               "Snakemake + denet native" = "#55A868")) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Disk write (MB)", fill = NULL,
       title = "C  Disk write per step",
       subtitle = paste0("mean +/- 1 SD, n = ", n_repeats, " repeats")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))
```

```{r fig-cpu-timeseries}
p_cpu <- ggplot(denet_ts,
                aes(x = time_s, y = cpu_usage + 1,
                    color = step_label, group = repeat_id)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.35, linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 0.6) +
  facet_wrap(~ step_label, scales = "free_x", nrow = 1,
             labeller = labeller(step_label = ts_facet_labels)) +
  scale_x_continuous(n.breaks = 4) +
  scale_y_continuous(trans = "log10",
                     labels = function(x) round(x - 1, 0)) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  labs(x = "Elapsed time (s)", y = "CPU usage (%)",
       title = "D  CPU usage per rule (all repeats)") +
  guides(color = "none") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 7),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        panel.grid = element_blank())
```

```{r fig-mem-timeseries}
p_mem <- ggplot(denet_ts,
                aes(x = time_s, y = mem_rss_mb,
                    color = step_label, group = repeat_id)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.35, linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~ step_label, scales = "free_x", nrow = 1,
             labeller = labeller(step_label = ts_facet_labels)) +
  scale_x_continuous(n.breaks = 4) +
  scale_y_log10(labels = label_number(accuracy = 1)) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  labs(x = "Elapsed time (s)", y = "RSS (MB)",
       title = "E  Memory usage per rule (all repeats)") +
  guides(color = "none") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 7),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        panel.grid = element_blank())
```

```{r fig-io-timeseries}
p_io <- ggplot(denet_ts,
               aes(x = time_s, y = disk_write_mb,
                   color = step_label, group = repeat_id)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.35, linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~ step_label, scales = "free_x", nrow = 1,
             labeller = labeller(step_label = ts_facet_labels)) +
  scale_x_continuous(n.breaks = 4) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_number(accuracy = 1)) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  labs(x = "Elapsed time (s)", y = "Disk write (MB, cumulative)",
       title = "F  Disk write per rule (all repeats)") +
  guides(color = "none") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 7),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        panel.grid = element_blank())
```

```{r combined-figure, fig.cap="Resource profiling of a simulated read alignment workflow. (A, B, C) Mean wall-clock time, peak RSS, and disk bytes written per pipeline step under three conditions: Snakemake (baseline, no denet), Snakemake + denet wrap (denet attached to each rule subprocess via a shell wrapper), and Snakemake + denet native (denet on PATH, no explicit wrap, rules run unmodified). Error bars are +/- 1 SD across benchmark repeats. SD was chosen over SE or CV to express absolute run-to-run variability in interpretable units. (D, E, F) CPU, RSS, and cumulative disk-write timeseries recorded by denet at 50 ms intervals for the wrap condition, faceted by rule in pipeline order. Elapsed time is relative to the start of each repeat."}
fig <- (p_time | p_rss | p_io_bar) / (p_cpu | p_mem | p_io) +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        plot.margin = margin(2, 2, 2, 2),
        panel.spacing = unit(0.3, "lines"))

dir.create("figures", showWarnings = FALSE)
ggsave("figures/denet_benchmark.pdf", fig, width = 12, height = 8)

fig
```
